summary: Implemented LOOP-03 direct Codex/Claude MCP parity proof v2. Replaced self-authored proof JSON with one-time challenge, MCP clientInfo, direct-parent process identity, five canonical tool receipts bound to one task, local identity HMAC, replay/tamper/stale/foreign identity rejection, strict both-client release parity, Observatory visibility, installer proof launchers, and purge cleanup. All local/adversarial regressions passed; fresh real Codex and Claude proofs remain pending because this running client predates the new MCP tool surface.
decisions: Release parity requires fresh v2 proofs from both real clients; Claude not_configured is diagnostic only.
The required direct tool set includes os_begin_task, get_context_pack, wiki_search, search_memory, and finish_task.
A synthetic stdio client or shell-launched server is rejected because Codex or Claude must be the MCP server direct parent.
Proof records retain hashes and bounded executable metadata, never raw prompts, queries, results, command lines, or executable paths.
The running client cannot self-certify after this build because its MCP server process predates the new begin/finalize tools.
next_steps: Install or update to app commit ac9f76f, fully restart Codex Desktop, and run DinoBrain Codex MCP Proof.cmd.
On a machine with Claude Code, fully restart Claude and run DinoBrain Claude MCP Proof.cmd.
Continue Phase 2 MEM-01 node lifecycle implementation while preserving the pending live-client acceptance gate.
